steps.narrate: The Authority built the funeral before it captured Tobias. That was how certain it had become.
White stone, black restraints, and a ring of fire waiting to burn a name after the memories were gone.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: The platform is keyed to him. If we break it, the extraction will choose another witness.

steps.narrate: Tobias stepped onto the platform before the Erasers could drag him there.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: A record is not the person who writes it. That is the point, Arrel. It must survive my handwriting.

steps.narrate: The first ring took the way he sharpened a pencil. The second reached for Elia's name.

choice.text: Burn the duty to leave a witness. Break the platform and pull Tobias out alive.
Keep the duty. Trust Tobias's record and let his chosen testimony finish.

steps.narrate: [ The fire waits for a name. ]
The duty became one white-gold impact. The platform forgot Tobias was its subject and released every restraint at once.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: You absolute illiterate. Remind me to thank you after I relearn gratitude.

steps.narrate: Arrel kept the duty. Tobias kept the platform's attention on himself while Elia carried the completed notebook beyond the square.

steps.speaker: Tobias
steps.text: Tell the truth badly if you must. Just do not let them become the only editors.

steps.narrate: The last ring took his name. The notebook did not go blank.
Tobias stumbled from the broken platform alive, missing several small habits and none of his anger.
The crowd remembered that someone had stood on the platform. No one but Arrel and Elia remembered who.
Beyond the square, a black monolith cut the eastern sky from horizon to cloud.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: Then we carry it east. Not as heat. Not yet.

steps.narrate: The monolith answered with a line of violet light.
